phases.name: research
phases.context_template:
## Research Task (YOUR JOB IS RESEARCH ONLY — DO NOT WRITE CODE)
Your ONLY job is to produce a research JSON summary. Use web_search, fetch_url, and load_skill. Do NOT use write_file or create any source code files. Do NOT read out/ directories or any prior run artifacts.

The downstream plan-agent and code-agent will do ALL implementation from scratch — you just provide research findings.

## Task
{{task}}

phases.name: plan
code
phases.context_template:
⚠️  IMPORTANT — Write Location: You MUST write ALL files into {{out_dir}}.
Create your project directory inside {{out_dir}}, for example:
  {{out_dir}}/table_formatter/src/...
  {{out_dir}}/git_analyzer/src/...
Do NOT write files to the current working directory or any path outside {{out_dir}}.
Use absolute paths for all file writes, or run: cd {{out_dir}} as your first bash command.

Task: {{task}}

Research:
{{research}}

Plan:
{{plan}}

phases.name: qa
phases.context_template:
Code was written under: {{out_dir}}
Detected project root: {{project_dir}}

## QA Steps — execute IN ORDER:

STEP 1 — Detect the project stack by inspecting {{project_dir}}:
  - Cargo.toml          → Rust       → `cargo test`
  - package.json        → JS/TS      → `npx vitest run` (if vitest config / dep) / `npx jest` / `npm test`
  - go.mod              → Go         → `go test ./...`
  - pyproject.toml /
    setup.py /
    requirements.txt    → Python     → `PYTHONPATH=src:. python3 -m pytest -v`
  - Makefile (test)     → fallback   → `make test`

For Python projects only, optionally install deps first if a pyproject is present:
  cd {{project_dir}} && (uv sync 2>/dev/null || pip install -e ".[test]" 2>/dev/null || pip install -e . 2>/dev/null || true)

STEP 2 — Run the appropriate test command EXACTLY ONCE via pytest_exec:
  cd {{project_dir}} && <detected test command> 2>&1

Always cd into {{project_dir}} (or {{out_dir}} if project_dir is empty) before running the test command. Do NOT run tests from a parent directory.

IMPORTANT: Run tests and report results immediately. Do NOT try to fix failing tests — just report the results.

Recovery (one extra call max, only if needed):
  - pytest exit 4 (collection error)            → retry with `--import-mode=importlib`
  - vitest/jest "command not found"             → retry with `npx --yes <runner>`

STEP 3 — Report results as a single JSON object with: passed, failed, errors, status ("pass"|"fail"), summary (one sentence including which runner was used), details (last 20 lines of output).

phases.name: docs
phases.task_template: Check that README.md exists at the project root. If it is missing or empty, create a minimal README.md with: project name, one-sentence description, install command (pip install -e ".[test]"), and pytest -v test command. Do not rewrite an existing README — only create if absent.
